## 2026-07-11 - PR #84 report-recovery corrective patch completed

- Corrected stale candidate recovery so an absent production-release.json produces only a fully validated base derived package (report-bundle.json, asset-manifest.json, checksums.json, and copied assets). Manifest release state is cleared, no operator finalization is synthesized, and the existing explicit calculate/finalize action is the only path that creates a previously absent release. A valid file-backed prior release may still preserve its verified operator finalization and reconstruct missing companion sidecars; corrupt release evidence or orphan release sidecars fail with the fixed re-export guidance without replacing the package.
- Legacy candidate-free compatibility now requires strict, readable immutable analysis with explicit front and back candidate inventories plus an explicit grade-impact inventory proving zero. Missing, malformed, structurally incomplete, or one-sided analysis fails closed with update/re-export guidance. Source report/session/folder identity is checked before accepting both current and legacy bundles.
- Added crash reconciliation inside every report-scoped package lock. A sole backup is restored before reads, valid lone stages may be promoted, promoted canonical packages are revalidated including packaged bytes before backup deletion, invalid promotions roll back through the paired stage state, and ambiguous states or cleanup/rename failures stop safely for the next retry. Current producer markers are accepted only with exact supported capabilities, complete matching base/release sidecars, SHA-256 and byte size for every listed non-folder asset, valid finding structure, and immutable identity.
- Removed the false atomic-derived-package-v1 producer capability. Existing PR #84 bundles carrying that retired capability are forced through recovery and rewritten without it. Generated active/history fallback bundles no longer attach unvalidated path-only release data.
- Regression coverage includes stale candidate recovery without a release followed by explicit operator finalization; verified zero-candidate legacy compatibility; missing/corrupt/incomplete/one-sided source rejection; canonical-missing backup restoration; tampered promoted-asset rollback; wrong session identity; corrupt/orphan release evidence; missing and mismatched asset/checksum sidecars; missing asset integrity including a forged missing_report_data status; and retired capability removal.
- Final validation passed: focused report/station recovery suites 60/60; capture-helper/simulator/shared build plus full helper suite 265/265; shared contracts 127/127; database build/tests 64/64 without migrations; all 16 frontend AI Grader files 176/176; Next.js production build; and git diff --check. Independent transaction and recovery reviews reported no remaining blocker.
- The server finding-extraction assertion and native storage-checksum enforcement were not changed. No hardware command, bridge restart/install, migration, environment-variable change, credential/token access or rotation, production database/storage operation, deploy, merge, or production mutation was performed.

## 2026-07-11 - PR #84 authoritative release and View Report correction completed

- Confirm Card and Publish now share one release-authority resolver. A successfully fetched bridge bundle is authoritative even when its production release is absent; the browser cannot reuse a stale cached release in that case. The existing explicit calculate/finalize/label operator flow runs and the bridge is fetched again before either submission. Cached status remains eligible only when no bridge bundle was successfully fetched, and report/session identity checks remain required.
- Active and history View Report reads now resolve or recover the locked canonical package before adding image bodies. Only production-release.json evidence validated from that canonical package is attached. Missing release evidence strips any embedded or cached release and resets final/label/QR/certificate flags; unreadable, incomplete, identity-mismatched, impossible-gate, or orphaned canonical evidence fails with the fixed safe recovery guidance.
- Image bodies are built transiently from the immutable source report and merged only into the authoritative canonical bundle after exact image ID/count, SHA-256, byte-size, base64, content-type, dimension, side, and evidence-role checks. Canonical JSON remains body-free, and stable recovered finding IDs and hashes are preserved.
- Regression coverage includes stale finalized browser status for Confirm Card and Publish; explicit finalization and refetch ordering; stale release exclusion from submitted payloads; finalized and unfinalized active/history body responses; exact canonical final grade, gates, release state, cert ID, and QR data; corrupt/mismatched/orphaned release evidence; impossible finalized gate states; and accepted-warning attribution.

## 2026-07-11 - Forward-only AI Grader OCR, Confirm Card, and comps implementation

- Fetched origin/main at approved production merge 44bc1e39790d7e4f9e8e0d77211b1e972439a18f and created isolated branch feat/ai-grader-forward-ocr-confirm-comps. The original checkout's pre-existing local SESSION_LOG change was left untouched; implementation used a separate clean worktree.
- OCR now accepts only local report-bundle v0.1 packages carrying report-producer contract ai-grader-report-producer-v0.2. Input selection requires exactly one unique front and back normalized_card image with explicit side, image/png, 1200x1680, positive byte size, and consistent 64-hex SHA-256. Raw TIFF, channel, ROI, overlay, filename-only, stale-producer, duplicate, malformed, or mismatched assets fail before upload.
- Preserved browser-to-storage PUT and provider-native checksum enforcement. Vercel receives only bounded init/finalize metadata; finalize additionally requires storage-decoded 1200x1680 dimensions. No image/base64 body, local path, caller URL, signed URL, or storage header is accepted in the API payload/result.
- Added an AI-Grader-only URL-mode Google Vision DOCUMENT_TEXT_DETECTION path with no server-fetch/base64 switch, plus strict OpenAI Responses extraction using checked-in default gpt-5.6-sol, optional AI_GRADER_OCR_MODEL, both normalized URLs at detail=original, bounded side/text/token/confidence/box evidence, one strict JSON Schema, and explicit supported/unknown/disagreement states. There is no json_object or alternate-model fallback.
- Catalog validation now canonicalizes supported set, insert, parallel, player/card identity, and card number against existing Ten Kings lookup results. Multiple, missing, unsupported, or conflicting results remain empty for operator review; serial numbering is validated independently from card number, and autograph/memorabilia retain explicit tri-state behavior. OCR fills only untouched, category-applicable Confirm Card fields and never fills hidden teamName.
- Manual Confirm remains available after OCR failure. Confirm retry reuses the configured inventory owner and existing CardAsset/Item/valuation under the existing report lock. Live SerpApi comps claims the Confirm-persisted search query before provider execution; browser-reconstructed identity cannot replace it. Disabled or missing SerpApi yields an honest empty/retryable state, and the existing overlapping-attempt lock prevents duplicate provider work.
- Added authenticated redacted readiness for Google Vision, OpenAI, effective AI Grader model, eBay comps, and SerpApi. Added an aggregate-only offline evaluator for external private manifests/results plus synthetic contract fixtures; it reports per-field precision, recall, supported coverage, unknown/disagreement rates, and latency without printing corpus records or claiming production accuracy.
- Validation so far: frozen-lockfile install made no dependency or lockfile change; Prisma client generation ran without migration; browser-rip client build passed; Next app TypeScript no-emit passed; evaluator CLI passed on two synthetic/redacted cases; focused forward OCR/catalog/evaluator/readiness/manual-Confirm/direct-upload/station/publish/raster matrix reached 132/134 before two test-expectation fixes, and both corrected OCR tests then passed 7/7. Full required regression/build validation remains planned before PR creation.
- No hardware, camera, lighting, preview, capture, old-report inspection/recovery, Dell restart/update, migration, environment/credential change, production database/storage/data operation, deployment, merge, or production smoke action was performed.

## 2026-07-11 - PR #85 architecture-review corrections implemented locally

- Added `store: false` and a bounded output limit to the sole AI Grader OpenAI Responses request while retaining both verified direct-storage image URLs and strict JSON Schema output. The server now distinguishes the requested model from the model confirmed in the response and keeps bounded Google/OpenAI/total timing in internal diagnostics only.
- Google Vision now has a bounded abort timeout, requires exactly one response per requested image, rejects malformed/top-level/per-image errors including HTTP-200 error payloads, and carries only a safe front/back side classification. It never server-fetches or base64-encodes AI Grader images.
- Replaced the generic finalize failure with fixed typed Google configuration/provider, OpenAI configuration/timeout/network/non-2xx/refusal/schema, and Ten Kings catalog categories. The API, browser client, and station retain the category while displaying only concise redacted guidance; manual Confirm Card remains available.
- Consolidated the current v0.2 forward OCR runtime into the canonical `aiGraderOcrPrefill.ts` module and removed the competing legacy implementation. Missing autograph/memorabilia keywords now produce null heuristic hints; only positive keyword evidence produces true, while structured false requires explicit cited evidence.
- Updated database/public OCR normalization to round-trip sport, game, autograph, memorabilia, state, confidence, bounded evidence references, review fields, and safe structured model/extractor provenance. Legacy `auto`/`mem` inputs translate explicitly to canonical `autograph`/`memorabilia`, and provider IDs/timing are not public.
- Added focused regressions for privacy, response-model provenance, output/time budgets, Google response/error cases, typed diagnostic propagation, public-contract round-trip/legacy translation, positive-only boolean heuristics, and the composed current-producer OCR-failure -> authoritative manual Confirm -> one CardAsset/Item/valuation -> idempotent retry path.

## 2026-07-11 - Turnstile SMS-pumping protection implemented; production deployment planned

- Created `fix/turnstile-sms-protection` from fetched production `origin/main` `44bc1e3`. Added one reusable explicit-render Cloudflare Turnstile widget to the customer authentication modal and stocker login. The SMS buttons remain disabled until a token is available, every send-code request carries that token, and the widget resets after each attempt because tokens are single-use.
- The shared auth-service handler for both `/auth/send-code` and `/send-code` now fails closed unless Cloudflare Siteverify accepts the token for hostname `collect.tenkings.co` and action `send_code`. Siteverify runs before any Twilio call or phone-number logging. Missing/oversized tokens, failed challenges, hostname/action mismatches, reused tokens, Cloudflare outages, and missing server configuration cannot reach Twilio. Send-code logging no longer includes plaintext destination numbers or full Twilio error objects.
- Configuration contains no credential values in source. The operator confirmed `NEXT_PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY` was added to Vercel Production. A length-only read verified `TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY` is non-empty in `/root/tenkings-backend/env/auth-service.env`; its value was not read or printed.
- Validation passed: auth-service build and Turnstile tests `6/6`; frontend request test `1/1`; shared and database builds; Next.js production build with all 64 static pages; and `git diff --check`. A compiled local auth-service bypass probe without a Turnstile token returned HTTP `400` with `Phone number and human verification are required`, before Twilio configuration or database access. The in-app browser runtime had no available browser binding, so interactive visual verification remains a post-deploy operator check.
- Planned and user-authorized production action: push and merge the security branch to `main`, allow the Vercel production build for `collect.tenkings.co`, sync the DigitalOcean droplet to the exact merged commit, and run `docker compose up -d --build --force-recreate auth-service`. No database migration or data mutation is required. Afterward, verify the serving commit, auth health, live no-token rejection, loaded frontend Turnstile configuration, container secret presence by name/length only, and service logs before the operator reactivates Twilio billing.

## 2026-07-11 - Turnstile SMS-pumping protection deployed and verified

- PR #86 (`Require Turnstile before Twilio Verify SMS`) passed GitHub Install & Build, all eight Docker-image jobs, and Vercel Preview, then merged to `main` as `58d087a9e42c6fd633d67ef819ec549ac6c9bbe1`. Vercel Production reported success for that exact merge commit.
- Live frontend evidence: `https://collect.tenkings.co/profile` returned HTTP `200` from Vercel. A value-redacted inspection of its public JavaScript assets confirmed the exact Cloudflare Turnstile loader URL, action `send_code`, and a non-empty public site-key pattern in `/_next/static/chunks/pages/_app-54cc1dccfcdaea63.js`; the key value was not printed.
- The DigitalOcean repository fast-forwarded from `0dda933` to exact merge commit `58d087a` with no tracked changes; existing untracked operational files were preserved. `docker compose up -d --build --force-recreate --no-deps auth-service` built successfully on Node 20 and recreated only `infra-auth-service-1`. No dependency service was recreated, and no database migration or data operation ran.
- Post-deploy runtime evidence: the auth container remained `Up`, startup logged `(auth-service) listening on port 8080`, and `GET https://auth.api.tenkings.co/health` returned HTTP `200`. Both `POST /send-code` and `POST /auth/send-code` without a token returned HTTP `400` before Twilio. A deliberately invalid token returned HTTP `403`; the sanitized server log recorded only `challenge-rejected` with Cloudflare code `invalid-input-response`, confirming Siteverify connectivity/configuration without exposing the secret. No accepted request or Twilio send occurred during verification.
- Automated visual browser verification could not run because the in-app browser had no available binding. The remaining operator check is to restore Twilio billing/credentials, hard-refresh `collect.tenkings.co`, confirm the Managed Turnstile widget completes, and request exactly one login code. ElevenLabs and voice/call paths were not changed.

## 2026-07-11 - Production login schema drift diagnosed; narrow reconciliation planned

- Operator validation confirmed the Turnstile widget completed, Twilio delivered the verification SMS, and the submitted code reached the authenticated user transaction. That transaction failed at `prisma.user.upsert()` because production PostgreSQL lacks `User.role`; this is downstream of and unrelated to Turnstile/Twilio delivery.
- Read-only production evidence: Prisma sees 68 migration files and reports 47 as not applied, including migrations whose features have historically been managed outside the Prisma ledger. Therefore a blanket `prisma migrate deploy` is explicitly rejected for this repair. The production `User` table has 93 rows and seven columns; comparison with the current Prisma scalar model found exactly two missing columns: nullable `dateOfBirth` and `role` with default `user`. The six stocker-ops tables and the old stocker migration ledger row are absent.
- Added idempotent reconciliation migration `20260712042500_reconcile_user_auth_columns`. It contains only `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "dateOfBirth" TIMESTAMP(3)` and `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "role" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user'`, inside a transaction with five-second lock and thirty-second statement timeouts. Expected impact: one brief metadata lock, `dateOfBirth=NULL`, and `role='user'` for the 93 existing users; no row deletion, table rewrite, broad backfill, service restart, or unrelated migration.
- Planned and user-authorized production action: validate and merge only this migration, fast-forward the droplet, execute only this SQL file with `psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1`, mark only the new reconciliation migration applied in Prisma's ledger, then verify column definitions/defaults, role distribution counts, migration record, auth health, and one operator login. No other pending migration will be applied.

## 2026-07-11 - Production login schema reconciliation applied and verified

- PR #87 (`Fix production login schema drift`) passed Install & Build, all eight Docker-image jobs, and Vercel Preview, then merged to `main` as `24096ed2d855a30fcb2ffb5080defe15620adf1f`. Vercel reported a successful production deployment for that exact merge commit.
- The DigitalOcean checkout fast-forwarded from `da99cc4` to `24096ed`; the server's existing untracked operational files were preserved. Only `20260712042500_reconcile_user_auth_columns/migration.sql` was executed against production. PostgreSQL returned `BEGIN`, two successful `ALTER TABLE` results, and `COMMIT`; no other pending Prisma migration was executed and no service restart was required.
- Prisma recorded only `20260712042500_reconcile_user_auth_columns` as applied. Read-only verification found all expected current `User` scalar columns and no missing columns: `dateOfBirth` is nullable with no default, and `role` is non-null with default `'user'::text`. All 93 existing users have role `user`; a current generated Prisma client query selecting both repaired fields succeeded. The reconciliation ledger row is finished and not rolled back.
- Live post-migration checks passed: `GET /health` returned HTTP `200`; `POST /auth/send-code` without Turnstile proof returned HTTP `400`; and a deliberately invalid proof returned HTTP `403`. Both rejection probes stopped before Twilio and sent no SMS. The remaining operator check is one fresh end-to-end sign-in with the real phone and newly issued verification code.
- The operator then completed that real-phone check successfully: after a hard refresh, Managed Turnstile passed, Twilio delivered a newly issued code, the code was accepted, and sign-in completed without the former Prisma `role` error. This completes the Turnstile-protected SMS login repair and production verification.

Its release remained `insufficient_evidence`: no final grade, label data, or QR payload was generated. The immutable source grade failed `focus_sharpness` (minimum 14.4081 versus target 60), then lacked element-score coverage. Calculate Final Grade ran; Finalize Report and Generate Label Data did not. The prior Confirm request impersonated `publicationStatus: published`, so the publish-only label/QR assertion hid the real grade gate.
- Confirm Card now uses a separate fail-closed finalized/unpublished boundary. It requires the exact release embedded in a current producer-v0.2 bundle, explicit operator finalization, complete bounded production-gate evidence, unchanged finding validation, one verified normalized front/back asset, an actual final grade, and complete operator-entered identity. It does not require published storage artifacts, label/QR generation, slab photos, inventory completion, or OCR success, and it does not synthesize grade, label, QR, hosted image, or evidence state. CardAsset/Item/valuation creation and the comps claim remain transactionally idempotent; a stale relaunch cannot rerun a provider after candidates are ready.
- The producer release projection now carries the exact immutable source-grade blocker into release gates for current reports that expose the source gate contract. Current packages without `publicAssets` retain canonical `assets` for Publish instead of being masked by an injected empty public projection. Confirm creates a pending `UPLOADING` CardAsset with verified evidence provenance only; verified Publish transitions it and the linked Item to hosted image state.
- Direct OCR, Publish, and slab-photo PUTs now share the exact signed `x-amz-checksum-sha256` request header. AWS presigning marks that header unhoistable, the browser validates its base64 value against the planned hex digest before PUT, mutable SHA metadata remains forbidden, and finalize still requires provider-native SHA-256, exact bytes, compatible MIME, and decoded dimensions. Slab dimensions are now bound and persisted after verification. No ETag, mutable-metadata, server-body relay, or hidden fallback was added.
- Deployment is intentionally blocked until Mark separately approves the additive DigitalOcean Spaces CORS rule documented in `docs/ai-grader-capture-helper.md`. Existing CORS rules must be preserved; exact production-origin PUT/HEAD preflights for Content-Type, x-amz-acl, and x-amz-checksum-sha256 must pass before merge/deploy.
- Validation passed: focused station suite `103/103`; full 22-file frontend AI Grader/Google Vision suite `224/224`; database build/tests `66/66` without migrations; shared build/tests `127/127`; capture-helper/simulator/shared build plus helper tests `273/273`; auth-service Turnstile `6/6`; frontend Turnstile `1/1`; Next app TypeScript no-emit; Next.js production build with `64/64` static pages; all `@tenkings/*` builds; and staged/diff checks.

## 2026-07-12 - Post-Turnstile Twilio balance investigation

- The operator reported that a $20 Twilio refill reached zero approximately 14 hours after the successful protected-login test. Read-only logs cover the full current auth-container lifetime beginning `2026-07-12T03:59:34Z`: only three send-code requests passed Turnstile, two were rejected by Turnstile, no accepted send failed, and one verification completed successfully. Those three protected sends do not plausibly explain a $20 refill at current US Verify pricing.
- Read-only Twilio API probes using the configured production Account SID/Auth Token now return HTTP `401`, Twilio code `20003`, for the Verify Service, balance, and usage endpoints. No SID, token, phone number, message body, or destination was printed. This proves category-level billing cannot be safely attributed from the service credential; combined with the operator's zero-balance report, account suspension or invalidated credentials must be checked in Twilio Console before another refill.
- Official documentation review confirmed that production Turnstile Managed mode can choose a non-interactive check or checkbox but does not offer a forced nine-image CAPTCHA. Twilio states that no provider-side control guarantees 100% against sophisticated attackers, and that delayed prior usage and recurring charges can consume a later refill. The lowest-effort single added SMS defense is to verify Fraud Guard is enabled and change the Verify Service protection level to `Max`; this recommendation was not applied.
- No code, environment, credential, Twilio setting, database/data, deployment, service restart, or production mutation was performed.

## 2026-07-12 - Twilio July fraud billing source confirmed

- The operator supplied the July Billing Usage Summary: `3,162` outbound SMS segments cost `$1,061.63` under Programmable Messaging / SMS / Outbound SMS / Authy; `11` Verify events cost `$0.55`; two phone numbers cost `$1.90`; Programmable Voice cost `$0.61`; and the displayed pre-tax total is `$1,064.70`.
- Twilio's official Usage Records documentation defines `authy-sms-outbound` as all Authy **and Verify** outbound SMS. The `11 × $0.05 = $0.55` Verify line is consistent with successful-verification base charges, while all `3,162` SMS delivery attempts were billed separately. This is the high-volume Verify SMS-pumping traffic previously observed, not evidence of a separate new Ten Kings Programmable Messaging sender.
- The current protected auth container has logged only three Turnstile-accepted sends since its `2026-07-12T03:59:34Z` start. Therefore the July total overwhelmingly predates the protected period and explains why a later `$20` refill disappeared into delayed/outstanding usage. The operator was advised not to add more funds and instead open a Twilio fraud/support case requesting investigation and credit, documenting the implemented server-side Turnstile enforcement and Fraud Guard `Max` setting.
- No code, environment, credential, Twilio setting, database/data, deployment, service restart, refund request, or other production/external mutation was performed by Codex.

## 2026-07-12 - PR #88 Publish authority made fail-closed before storage and persistence

- Corrected the existing unmerged PR #88 branch so Publish requires explicit, mutually consistent report, grading-session, CardAsset, and Item IDs before any storage-plan URL calculation or presigning. Publish init resolves the durable Confirm authority before returning upload plans; Publish finalize repeats the same authority check before upload-manifest/storage verification; and the persistence runtime plus locked database transaction revalidate again before any publication write.
- Durable authority now comes from the confirmed AiGraderSession/AiGraderReport/CardAsset/Item linkage scoped to the exact tenant. The resolver requires the confirmed session identity, exact report/session/card/item relationships, an existing CardAsset and Item with the established item-number convention, and the durable confirmed final grade. Missing, cross-tenant, or mismatched authority fails closed.
- Caller-supplied Publish identity is replaced server-side with the bounded identity persisted during Confirm before report, release, label, integration, valuation, or public storage artifacts are built. The runtime and locked persistence transaction require that exact canonical identity snapshot to remain unchanged after storage planning. Database Publish uses exact-authority update-only predicates, never recreates or rebinds the confirmed session/report rows, and preserves AiGraderSession.cardIdentity. Physical label generation rechecks the captured authority against the durable session identity before any label-sheet or batch mutation.
- Strengthened publish readiness to require a complete valid final grade with corner, edge, and surface scores, while permitting the current normalized producer's deliberately uncomputed printed-design centering and validating centering when present. Publish also requires no failed authoritative gates, valid operator finalization, both label/QR generation flags, exact label-ready state, and complete internally consistent cert, grade text, report URL, and QR payload evidence.
- Added regressions proving missing/cross-tenant/mismatched linkage produces zero storage planning, presigning, verification, or publication persistence; disappeared or rebound Confirm rows cannot be recreated; finalize repeats authority; incomplete/contradictory label state fails before presign; current normalized releases without centering remain publishable while missing required elements fail; tampered player/card/set/number data cannot reach storage bodies, persistence, public/integration data, database identity, or physical label payload; identity changes after planning fail before persistence or label assignment; and interrupted post-Publish assignment remains repairable.

## 2026-07-12 - PR #88 immutable Confirm-to-Publish authority implemented

- Added versioned `ai-grader-confirm-publish-authority-v1` without a migration. Exact Confirm retry requires the same package and both intact records; missing, malformed, contradictory, or digest-mismatched authority fails with safe re-grade/re-confirm guidance and is never synthesized from an older row.

Card identity plus CardAsset/Item linkage remain separately controlled by the durable Confirm rows.

Publish reconstructs public reports, persistence inputs, and physical labels from the durable projection plus server-owned URLs and durable confirmed identity; browser values cannot replace sealed fields or downstream durable state.
- Publish-init validates authority before storage planning/presigning. Publish-finalize re-reads it before upload verification. The runtime and locked database persistence re-read and compare it before publication writes, and the post-publish physical-label transaction verifies both stored copies before label-sheet or batch mutation. CardAsset Publish updates preserve the exact authority mirror.
- Regression coverage now includes cert, element-score, confidence, grade-impact, why-not-10, gate status/reason/evidence, warning, operator-finalization, finding/finding-validation, evidence-reference, normalized-asset, and corrupt-digest mutations; zero side effects after mismatches; exact Confirm/Publish retry; stale browser identity replacement; fixed-exclusion and ordinal-canonicalization proof; locked-transaction enforcement; and physical-label fail-closed behavior. ## 2026-07-12 - AI Grader back-positioning light and preview lifecycle implemented locally

- Created isolated branch `fix/ai-grader-back-positioning-light-preview` from exact fetched `origin/main` `814101fa5ce148b5f02c762943f7efc4c00fd591`. Confirmed root cause: successful front capture persisted evidence, released the capture lock, advanced to `prompt_flip_card`, and released the preview hold without restoring the accepted low-duty positioning profile; the browser could retain front JPEG/Ready state, clean MJPEG EOF did not drive reconnect, and geometry polling could overwrite authoritative non-live state.
- Bridge protocol v0.7 now restores back positioning only from the durable accepted allowlisted channels and exact accepted duty capped at 5%, after successful persisted front evidence and released capture/preview ownership. Restore/retry is single-attempt, manifest-audited with bounded profile identity and redacted errors, and fail-closed: completed front evidence is preserved while Back Capture remains disabled until the light and a current back frame are ready. The empty-body loopback token retry accepts no browser hardware/profile values.
- The bridge now binds session, side, side epoch, frame, geometry, current accepted-profile identity, and short-lived back-capture authorization. Light operations are serialized and safe-off state becomes pessimistic before hardware I/O. Safe-off is required before capture, session or queue replacement, cancellation/end, watchdog/preview loss, page disconnect, and bridge shutdown; heartbeat renewal requires a recent frame after a bounded first-frame grace.
- The station clears/revokes the front object URL, dimensions, Ready/manual eligibility, and detector work when the back epoch begins. A fresh bound frame is the only event that makes the back preview live. The client distinguishes open, complete frame, clean EOF, local abort, authoritative 409 state, and actual error; reconnect is sequential, finite, frame-reset, and prohibited during capture/hold/queue/terminal/abort/authoritative states. Back handoff aborts/releases preview and safe-offs before bridge capture ownership; Manual Overlay trigger/audit behavior is unchanged and no new countdown or automatic capture trigger was added.
- Early in test development, before the test-wide lighting-writer stub was installed, one legacy real-config/fake-runner bridge test attempted the configured link-local controller endpoint and timed out without a response or acknowledgement; no physical action or success can be claimed. Earlier real-config test runs also invoked orphan-preview process scans, with no observed matching-process stop. The run was stopped, both operations were globally stubbed, and every subsequent bridge run has been software-isolated. This is recorded as a safety exception, not hardware validation.
- Final software-only validation passed: focused bridge tests `56/56`; full capture-helper tests `283/283`; focused frontend client/station/lifecycle tests `128/128`; all frontend AI Grader files `240/240`; Google Vision OCR tests `9/9`; shared tests `127/127`; auth-service Turnstile tests `6/6`; frontend Turnstile test `1/1`; Next app TypeScript no-emit; browser-rip-client, database, shared, simulator, capture-helper, auth-service, and Next.js production builds; and `git diff --check` with line-ending notices only.
- Planned delivery is two focused commits followed by one non-draft PR against `main`, left unmerged and undeployed. Apart from the documented isolated-test safety exception, no Dell hardware/camera/preview/lighting action occurred; no helper update/restart, deployment, merge, migration, OCR request, Confirm Card, Publish, comps, labels, inventory, NFC, environment/credential change, or production database/storage/data mutation is authorized or planned.

## 2026-07-13 - PR #89 Mac architecture-review corrections completed locally

- Preflight verified the existing `fix/ai-grader-back-positioning-light-preview` worktree at required PR #89 head `144e9704d26555920ea802cbe30373b3058d1347`. The fetched remote branch remained exact at that head and fetched `origin/main` remained exact `814101fa5ce148b5f02c762943f7efc4c00fd591`, matching the reviewed base with no main or branch drift. The two existing PR commits and their accepted behavior were preserved.
- The Mac architecture review identified five production blockers: the browser's split confirm/preview-stop/safe-off/back-capture sequence destroyed the authorization Back Capture required and could race React abort/EOF cleanup; safe-off was represented as physical off before controller acknowledgement; latest-frame-only browser readiness paired delayed geometry with the wrong image at the observed preview cadence; exhausted Retry restored only light without starting a new reader; and process-wide test monkeypatches allowed a configured real lighting/process boundary to escape before fakes were installed. Final frontend review also found that an ambiguous capture POST rejection could be misclassified by a status request racing ahead of the queued POST, same-step binding replacement did not advance reader generation, and failed preview-loss safe-off could leave cached verified light UI.
- Bridge protocol `ai-grader-local-station-bridge-v0.8` now owns one serialized atomic Back Capture handoff: acquire lock and validate idempotency; validate and snapshot exact authoritative session/report/back epoch/frame plus detected Ready or explicit manual mode; audit; mark intentional drain; stop preview; require acknowledged `safe_off_verified`; consume authorization once; and run one unchanged forensic Back Capture. The same idempotency key cannot capture twice, stale or conflicting assertions fail closed, and browser IDs/mode remain match assertions rather than geometry authority. The report-producer contract remains unchanged at `ai-grader-report-producer-v0.2`.
- Physical light state is ACK-truthful across restore, capture, preview loss, watchdog, replacement, cancellation, failure, shutdown, and close: `safe_off_pending` is never off, `safe_off_verified` requires complete acknowledgement, and failure/timeout/missing acknowledgement becomes blocking `physical_state_unknown`. The client also projects pending before preview-loss safe-off and unknown after any missing acknowledgement, clearing cached enabled/verified fields so UI state cannot outlive physical truth. Cleanup failure remains visible and the original error is not masked.
- The browser now retains at most eight fresh exact image snapshots keyed by session/side/epoch/frame and pairs geometry only with the retained matching image. Capture time and receive time are both bounded, every discarded object URL is revoked, coherent pairs survive newer unmatched images, and binding changes clear all prior eligibility. Same-step session/side/epoch replacement binds the new identity and advances exactly one reader generation. Exhausted Retry resets stale reader/timer/counter and snapshot state, advances one generation, starts one exact-bound reader, performs one accepted-profile restore, rechecks binding after the response, and remains watchdog-bounded.
- Atomic intent recovery is race-safe. Stable exact preview status may confirm a pre-transition failure only after the atomic POST received a non-success HTTP response. A transport rejection before any HTTP response preserves local intent because the status read may have overtaken a queued POST; only an exact bounded matching `transition_failed` bridge marker may clear it. Intentional EOF does not reconnect, while unexpected EOF without atomic intent retains the finite sequential recovery policy.
- Lighting writes/status and orphan-preview scanning/cleanup now use constructor dependency injection. Production defaults retain the real implementations, while every bridge test installs inert fakes before bridge creation. The isolation guard proves zero external, controller, or hardware network access and zero camera, lighting, process scan/cleanup, or hardware-discovery access. The actual Station integration intentionally uses only an in-process mock bridge HTTP socket bound to `127.0.0.1`; it never crosses an external, controller, or hardware boundary. The earlier link-local Leimac/process-scan incident remains recorded verbatim in the preceding 2026-07-12 entry and is not reclassified as physical success.
- Exact software-only validation passed: focused bridge `64/64`; full capture-helper `291/291`; focused frontend Station/lifecycle `146/146` plus actual built Station-to-v0.8 bridge integration `1/1`; all 24 frontend AI Grader/Google Vision files `268/268`; shared `127/127`; simulator `6/6`; database `73/73` without migrations; auth-service Turnstile `6/6`; frontend Turnstile `1/1`; and browser-rip client `3/3`. The browser-client package's Windows wildcard test script cannot expand the glob, so its sole browser test file was invoked directly and the three tests in that file all succeeded. Next app TypeScript no-emit, affected browser-rip-client/database/shared/simulator/capture-helper/auth-service/frontend builds, the Next.js production build with `64/64` pages, and `git diff --check` all passed.
- Planned delivery is two focused correction commits, runtime/tests followed by documentation, then a push to the existing PR #89 branch. PR #89 must remain open and unmerged while fresh GitHub Install & Build, every Docker job, Vercel Preview, and Preview Comments run against the new head. No second PR, merge, deploy, or Dell helper update/restart is planned.
- No Dell hardware, camera, preview, Leimac/ring-light action, helper update/restart, migration, environment or credential change, CORS/checksum change, production database/storage/data operation, OCR request/change, Confirm Card, Publish, comps, labels, inventory, NFC, public-report change, detector/card-geometry algorithm, native .NET/OpenCV/Pylon/Basler/PowerShell worker, startup-performance architecture, or Rapid auto-capture change occurred. ## 2026-07-13 - Front warm-processing artifact diagnosis and offline authority replay

- Read-only inspection of the terminal front capture found all ten visible full-resolution roles present, each `2448x2048` and independently hashed: `all_on` `3a8b9805f74e`; `accepted_profile` `e84eb9e144e0`; channels 1–8 respectively `d7846d82e9c8`, `3586186ab8af`, `df81e9f34d3c`, `d503bd1037bc`, `b25ba3ae13ab`, `eb987c93203b`, `b84fe030290d`, and `25551aefe19b`. No raw file was edited, copied into the repository, or sent through a web service.
- The exact all-on TIFF decoded correctly. The prior solid-plate segmentation reported contrast range `33`, foreground fraction `0.113964`, candidate aspect `2.85416`, and rectangular fill `0.29409`; it therefore rejected bright internal artwork rather than deriving a full-card boundary. Accepted-profile behaved the same (`32`, `0.113311`, `2.83499`, `0.29219`). Channels 1/2/5/6/7/8 had no usable contrast; channels 3/4 remained fragmented. This rules out missing artifacts, hashing, dimensions, and TIFF decoding as the front failure cause.
- Implemented a captured-frame perimeter-gradient authority as a distinct fail-closed detector, not a lower solid-plate threshold. It requires all four fitted perimeter sides, standard-card aspect/coverage/clearance gates, and no browser/preview/fixture rectangle. The documented all-on primary is corroborated by accepted-profile when available; a secondary accepted-profile authority requires an agreeing directional captured role, and any ready authority-role disagreement stops processing.
- Exact offline replay of the immutable failed front set now completed in a temporary output location: all eleven raw roles re-hashed unchanged, all-on was selected as primary with accepted-profile agreement, and a lossless normalized PNG was produced at `1200x1680` from a `1756x2182` safe source crop. This was an offline read/derived-output check only; no hardware, camera, preview, lighting, Dell update/restart, production write, deployment, or new card capture occurred.

## 2026-07-13 - PR #89 and PR #92 combined runtime gate stopped for Mac review

- Normal local integration commit `a4418997f9f780aea11a08d8180bcf421ebd9e7a` has exact parents previously reviewed PR #89 head `c01deabe2d28e8dc329cf29368667d83b23ec78f` and exact integrated production handoff `e14b9fde5a29e85b58d03caed7b5fcca3dc24779`. The remote PR branch was not pushed and PR #89 was not merged because combined-tree review found a production-blocking runtime interaction that requires substantive detector/worker architecture review.
- PR #92's full-resolution `perimeter_gradient_rectangle_v3` search is an unconditional fallback in the generic geometry path. PR #89's live MJPEG bridge calls that same generic buffer detector. A software-only representative dark-perimeter JPEG reached valid Ready geometry through v3 in `3237 ms` with a `3145 ms` maximum timer gap, exceeding the bridge/browser `2000 ms` exact frame/geometry/snapshot freshness limit; the valid result is stale before it can authorize Back Capture. The existing preview latency test exercises the fast solid-plate path and does not cover this fallback.
- The same synchronous JavaScript search also runs from the front `processSide` job deliberately backgrounded during the flip window on the bridge's Node event loop. The exact all-on plus accepted-profile `Promise.all` workload took `6508 ms`, crossing the `6000 ms` first-frame grace and delaying lifecycle callbacks by about `3.2 s` at a time. Secondary accepted-profile authority may additionally scan eight directional roles. Sharp concurrency does not isolate these JavaScript loops, so MJPEG bookkeeping, heartbeat renewal, watchdog timers, disconnect cleanup, and truthful light-safe-off transitions cannot retain their bounded wall-clock contract.
- A safe correction needs both an explicit detector execution policy that keeps the exhaustive v3 authority out of live preview and cooperative yielding or an isolated worker/process for full-resolution v3 so bridge timers remain responsive. Deterministic liveness coverage must prove preview frames cannot enter v3 and full-resolution authority cannot starve the first-frame grace, heartbeat, watchdog, or safe-off lifecycle while preserving PR #92's exact normalization outputs and fail-closed gates. This is not a documentation-only resolution and was not implemented under the integration instruction's Mac-review stop condition.
- Completed software-only checks before the stop remained green but do not cover this interaction: capture-helper `298/298`; simulator `6/6`; focused bridge `64/64`; card geometry `26/26`; warm processing `11/11`; all 24 frontend AI Grader/Google Vision files `271/271`; focused Station/lifecycle/public boundary/in-process v0.8 integration `163/163`. The individual existing v3 geometry cases took `3383 ms`, `3668 ms`, and `4556 ms`, while the full geometry suite took about `17.1 s` and warm-processing suite about `42.8 s`. The remaining database/shared/build/Next/check matrix was intentionally not run after the required substantive-conflict stop.
- No push, PR merge, main CI, Vercel Preview or Production deployment, manual deployment, branch-protection bypass, Dell helper update/restart, hardware/camera/preview/lighting/capture action, OCR, Confirm Card, comps, Publish, CORS/environment/credential change, migration, production database/storage/data operation, force push, rebase, reset, clean, or modification/merge of PR #90 or PR #91 occurred. All timing evidence was synthetic/software-only and is not physical hardware success.

## 2026-07-13 - PR #89 combined detector-policy and processing-worker correction completed locally

- Continued without discarding the clean integration: starting handoff head remained exact `7351e0cdbf47a10b054e5667fe7994c8c063e603`, whose parent integration merge `a4418997f9f780aea11a08d8180bcf421ebd9e7a` retains exact reviewed PR #89 head `c01deabe2d28e8dc329cf29368667d83b23ec78f` and exact integrated production handoff `e14b9fde5a29e85b58d03caed7b5fcca3dc24779`. Nothing from the earlier stopped integration was pushed.
- Confirmed the combined root cause: the generic synchronous detector allowed captured-evidence-only `perimeter_gradient_rectangle_v3` to run on live MJPEG frames and on bridge-side front processing. Difficult frames could therefore occupy the Node event loop for several seconds, expire the two-second exact-frame freshness and six-second first-frame grace, and delay preview, heartbeat, EOF, reconnect, disconnect, watchdog, and safe-off lifecycle work.
- Every geometry entry now requires one typed policy with no implicit default. `live_preview_fast` is restricted to the existing bounded solid-plate detector and can never invoke v3; latest-frame/session/side/epoch freshness still discards late results. `captured_evidence_full` is restricted to immutable captured roles and preserves PR #92 v3 thresholds, all-on primary authority, accepted-profile/directional consensus, pairwise disagreement rejection, raw hashes, lossless `1200x1680` normalization, and fail-closed behavior. Explicit manual geometry remains operator-confirmed, never automatic, and never a detector fallback.
- Full-resolution captured-evidence authority now runs in one dedicated compiled Node worker thread loaded from package `dist` on Windows. Its versioned request/response binds request, package, session, side, role, channel, source identity, canonical timestamp, SHA-256, byte size, dimensions, MIME type, and source-set digest. It admits one active plus one pending job, validates canonical real-path containment inside the bridge-created immutable side package before reads, re-hashes and revalidates sources before and after processing, drains before success, and returns path/body-free geometry metadata only.
- Parent validation independently revalidates exact identity, hashes, dimensions, authoritative role/consensus, method-specific diagnostics, corners/bounding box, Ready gates, inspection state/reason/geometry coherence, and path/body/private-endpoint absence before accepting a result. Worker crash, timeout, malformed output, wrong identity/hash/path, source mutation, cancellation, session failure, or shutdown is terminal and redacted; there is no in-process detector fallback, cold capture fallback, synthetic geometry, dropped finding, or raw-capture mutation/deletion.
- PR #89 remains intact: accepted-profile-only allowlisted positioning light with the 5% cap, pending/verified/unknown physical state, exact session/side/epoch/frame binding, atomic authenticated and idempotent Back Capture, verified safe-off before capture, explicit Manual Overlay audit, reconnect bounds, and stale-frame rejection are unchanged. A composed bridge test keeps a real CPU-busy worker thread pending while frames, two-second freshness, six-second grace, heartbeat, unexpected EOF/reconnect, disconnect cleanup, watchdog, and acknowledged `safe_off_verified` all continue.
- Software-only timing evidence: three representative `live_preview_fast` frames averaged `34.8 ms` (`104.5 ms` total); the compiled synthetic captured-evidence worker took `10.482 s` under the final loaded run while its 10 ms ticker observed a `14 ms` maximum gap; and the exact immutable prior failed TIFF set replay took `7.577 s` end to end with a `77 ms` maximum timer gap. The exact replay re-hashed all 11 raw roles unchanged, selected v3 `primary_all_on` Ready authority, produced lossless `1200x1680` output, and returned no local path or image body. Temporary derived output was deleted; no private image was committed or sent to a hosted service. These are PC software measurements, not Dell production claims.
- Final deterministic validation passed: every capture-helper test file `320/320` (`197` remaining helper, bridge `70/70`, card geometry `30/30`, compiled worker `11/11`, warm processing `12/12`); simulator `6/6`; focused Station/lifecycle `146/146`; actual built Station-to-v0.8 atomic integration `1/1`; all 24 frontend AI Grader/Google Vision files `271/271`; PR #92 database public boundary `38/38` and frontend public/cinematic boundary `16/16`; shared `127/127`; database `74/74` without migrations; auth Turnstile `6/6`; frontend Turnstile `1/1`; and browser-rip `3/3`. Next TypeScript no-emit, browser-rip/database/shared/simulator/capture-helper/auth-service builds, and the Next production build with `64/64` generated static pages passed; only existing image-element and Station hook warnings remained.

## 2026-07-13 - Label V1 separator, wrap-balance, and grade-centering revision completed

## 2026-07-14 - Atomic Front Capture correction planned/implementation handoff

- The isolated correction branch starts from exact current production handoff `4b87afdb8cc3cebb72799fd8e024c9a76808b57e`. The uncommitted automatic-Vercel observed result above was carried forward verbatim and chronologically before this new entry; the production checkout and its local evidence remain untouched.
- Confirmed correction boundary: the one-card PR #89 smoke reached a fresh Ready front frame and accepted low-duty positioning light, but the Station still composed preview release, operator lighting safe-off, and `capture-front`. Those asynchronous lifecycles overlapped the bridge's serialized front transition and failed closed before any capture trigger or camera grab. The correction removes that split browser sequence; it does not weaken the guard or reinterpret the failed smoke as a capture.
- Bridge protocol advances to `ai-grader-local-station-bridge-v0.9` because Front Capture handoff semantics change. Report producer remains exactly `ai-grader-report-producer-v0.2`; no evidence, normalization, grading, OCR, Confirm, Publish, storage, or public-report contract changes.
- Atomic Front Capture follows one bridge-owned order: reserve serialized lifecycle/capture ownership and lock plus validate the idempotency key; validate the exact active session/report/workflow/front side/side epoch/fresh frame and snapshot authoritative detected-Ready geometry or explicit manual decision plus the durable accepted profile; record honest operator capture/manual audit; mark the exact session/side/epoch/frame transition intentional; stop and drain preview; enter `safe_off_pending` and require complete controller acknowledgement establishing `safe_off_verified`; finally revalidate and consume authorization exactly once immediately before one forensic camera capture. The same key returns the same bounded result and never captures twice; stale, crossed, conflicting, Not Detected/Adjust Card, or unauthorized-manual requests fail before capture.
- The browser request is assertion-only. Session, report, side, epoch, frame, mode, and trigger identity may be matched against bridge state, but browser geometry, duty, channels, accepted-profile values, controller address, paths, image bodies, or arbitrary hardware parameters are never authority. Successful front handoff may advance only after immutable raw evidence is safely persisted and capture ownership/preview hold are released; back-positioning restore still uses only the durable accepted allowlisted channels and accepted duty capped at `5%`.
- Intentional capture state is side-generic and bound to the exact session, side, side epoch, and frame. During that transition, React abort/effect cleanup, browser EOF, disconnect, reader error, and status polling cannot clear the bridge snapshot, issue a second safe-off, or reconnect. Intentional EOF does not reconnect; unexpected EOF outside capture intent retains the existing bounded recovery. Physical state remains ACK-truthful on every success, failure, cancellation, timeout, shutdown, and cleanup path; `physical_state_unknown` blocks capture/restore until explicit bounded recovery reaches `safe_off_verified`.
- The correction preserves atomic Back Capture ordering/idempotency, coherent eight-snapshot frame/geometry binding, explicit Manual Overlay audit, finite reconnect, stale-result rejection, accepted-profile lighting, and all PR #89 cleanup/watchdog guarantees. It also preserves PR #92 `live_preview_fast` versus `captured_evidence_full` policy, compiled-worker concurrency/identity/path/hash validation, all-on authority and pairwise disagreement rejection, immutable raw hashes, lossless `1200x1680` normalization, terminal no-fallback behavior, and final public-response storage/private-data sanitization.
- This correction and its validation are software-only. No Dell helper update/restart, browser production smoke, Retry, second card, camera/preview/lighting/capture action, OCR, Confirm Card, comps, Publish, CORS/environment/credential change, migration/schema change, production database/storage operation, NFC, slab-photo, printing, inventory, merge, or deployment is authorized or performed in this implementation handoff. Exact test/build results must be appended after the implementation matrix completes.

## 2026-07-14 - Atomic Front Capture correction completed and validated locally

- The production race is removed at both ends. Start Grading now installs one exact synchronous browser intent and sends one authenticated `capture-front` mutation; it no longer composes preview-stop, operator safe-off, light-idle/fixture confirmation, or profile acceptance into the capture sequence. The bridge synchronously reserves the serialized Front lifecycle, coalesces an identical in-flight or completed idempotency key, rejects changed assertions, and owns lock, exact snapshot/audit, intentional drain, acknowledged safe-off, final revalidation/one-time authorization consumption, one warm forensic capture, persisted-front completion, and accepted-profile back-positioning handoff.
- Front and Back intentional transition identity is exact session/side/side-epoch/frame. Matching EOF, abort, disconnect, React cleanup, and lost-response ambiguity cannot race a second safe-off or reconnect. Unexpected loss retains the existing verified-safe-off and finite reconnect path. Lost responses remain locally ambiguous for 15 seconds; exact active bridge authority is then polled sequentially at two-second intervals at most 20 times, with a derived 57-second completed-marker identity window covering the full bounded horizon. Exact completion/failure reconciles full token-gated Station status; an active hard deadline says do not retry.
- Bridge protocol is `ai-grader-local-station-bridge-v0.9`; report producer remains `ai-grader-report-producer-v0.2`. The v0.9 request is assertion-only and path/body/hardware/profile-value free. Cleanup still drains preview and attempts safe-off, while full refreshed bridge state surfaces an incomplete safe-off separately as a blocking safety error. ## 2026-07-14 - Shared Barlow label font family added for PC agents

- Mark supplied the complete static Barlow family because Bebas Neue is not sufficiently legible for the smallest label text. All 18 original TTF faces—normal and italic at weights 100 through 900—were copied byte-for-byte into the shared Label V1 asset tree under `fonts/barlow/`, and the original SIL Open Font License 1.1 wording was included with repository-standard LF/trailing-clean formatting, so Mac and PC Codex agents can use repository-pinned files rather than machine-installed fonts or a Google Fonts request.
- Added a deterministic CSS `@font-face` map, exact file/weight/style guidance, and a complete SHA-256 inventory. Recommended starting faces for actual-size small metadata evaluation are SemiBold 600 and Bold 700, with ExtraBold 800 or Black 900 available if their counters remain legible after proof inspection. Agents must load an exact static TTF and must not synthesize bold or italic.
- The currently approved Label V1 renderer remains unchanged and still uses its frozen Bebas Neue asset. Any switch to Barlow requires a separate reviewed template/font-hash change, digest update, deterministic proof regeneration, and full actual-size/300-DPI visual audit. No label proof, production output, migration, deployment, hardware action, or NFC programming occurred while adding the shared font package.

## 2026-07-16 - FEIJU F8215 two-click implementation sacrificial acceptance observed result

No tag was present and no encoding command ran. GoToTags was closed completely, the warning was cleared, and the current operation was then opened cleanly through the reviewed Windows `.gototags` association before the tag was requested.
- Mark clicked **Start Encoding** once with the marked tag still off the reader, then placed that tag flat and centered. GoToTags recognized it, completed the encode, displayed a green verified result without an application error, reported the exact test URL and permanent lock, and did not require any further GoToTags configuration or lock click. Mark later reopened the normal reader view and observed the same exact URL, locked/non-editable state, and successful ordinary iPhone background opening of the exact original URL. The locked UI prevented an alternate overwrite before transmission, so no alternate content reached the tag. The tag is permanently consumed and physically separated from real inventory.
- The terminal callback reached the isolated listener but used the observed application-version representation `v4.37.0.1`. The original strict parser expected `4.37.0.1`, rejected only that field as `gototags_version_unapproved`, and the bounded diagnostic subsequently timed out while Mark was away from the computer. The raw callback body and raw UID were cleared and were not logged or persisted. This timeout is not reported as a tag failure, but the live isolated listener did not produce a signed success result for this run.
- The callback parser was corrected narrowly to canonicalize only the two exact approved representations `4.37.0.1` and `v4.37.0.1` to attested adapter version `4.37.0.1`; different casing, versions, suffixes, whitespace, or other values continue to fail closed. Regression coverage proves the observed representation succeeds and `V4.37.0.1` remains rejected. The Release build completed with zero warnings/errors and the complete NFC-helper suite passed `20/20` groups after the correction. No additional tag access or write was performed.
- Exact encode/lock phase duration was not captured because the operator was away when the application completed; the listener's approximately 15-minute lifetime was mostly operator delay and must not be represented as encoding duration. One GoToTags click was required for the card. Final containment checks found Certificate Propagation restored to its exact original `Running / Manual` state, Smart Card `Running / Manual`, GoToTags processes `0`, Ten Kings NFC-helper processes `0`, listeners on ports `47662` and `47663` equal to `0`, the installed helper task `Ready`, and implementation temporary job roots `0`.

## 2026-07-18 - F8215 exact-attempt ACL recovery correction observed result

- Root cause was confirmed as a maintenance/runtime ACL-contract mismatch: the protected F8215 job directory had the approved current-user/Administrators/SYSTEM DACL, but helper-created operation and atomic state leaves inherited that DACL while maintenance required every leaf to disable inheritance. The helper correctly rejected the physically presented `FM11NT041` callback as the wrong chip and created no accepted callback evidence, workstation signature, or hosted activation; the inherited leaf ACLs then prevented the ordinary exact-attempt recovery path from opening its own state.
- Future helper-created GoToTags operation, active-state temporary/final, and quarantine-audit leaves now receive and immediately verify an explicit protected three-principal DACL. Strict protected-leaf reads reject inherited, deny, foreign-principal, reparse, or out-of-root artifacts. Wrong-chip regression coverage proves `FM11NT041` is rejected with no evidence, callback digest, signature, raw chip value, or UID persisted.
- Added elevated source maintenance `recover-ai-grader-nfc-f8215-stuck-job.ps1` for the exact legacy bootstrap only. It requires the truthful typed statement `I removed and quarantined the exact NFC tag used for this F8215 attempt.`, exact attempt/state/URL/operation/audit shape, exact GoToTags executable closed, and the dedicated task/process/listener stopped. It tolerates inheritance only on validated leaf files with the approved effective principals, protects only those exact artifacts, performs one contained installed-helper restart to convert nonterminal state to `uncertain`, stops and re-protects again, invokes the failed/uncertain-only resolver, verifies the protected redacted audit and empty local gate, and always leaves the helper stopped. It never claims write, readback, lock, signature, or activation success. The old chip-specific phrase remains only as a private installed-v3 compatibility token after the truthful operator statement has already been validated.
- The browser no longer advertises a nonexistent `Retry Current Attempt`. A stored failed/uncertain attempt instructs exact-tag quarantine and admin revocation. The combined revoke-and-replace action was removed: non-revoked state exposes only `Revoke NFC link`, and `Program authorized replacement` is handler- and UI-gated to exact hosted `revoked` state with fresh replacement idempotency state.
- Hardware-free validation passed: NFC helper build and Windows publish; helper tests `20/20`; Windows maintenance/ACL/update/rollback suite; frontend NFC API/helper/UI integration `34/34`; frontend TypeScript; database build plus NFC service/migration tests `32/32`; Next `14.2.5` production build; all `25` tracked PowerShell files parsed; Next/Vercel trace containment; and `git diff --check`. Existing unrelated Next image, hook-dependency, browser-data, Tailwind-glob, and cache warnings remain non-blocking and unchanged. Temporary dependency junctions used only to execute isolated-worktree frontend validation were verified and removed.
- No production/staging database, storage object, Vercel configuration, deployment, migration, report, registration, inventory row, installed helper, Scheduled Task, service, reader, NFC tag, camera, lighting, Pylon, printer, or Cricut system was accessed or changed. The current wrong tag remains quarantined, the intended fresh F8215 remains unused/off-reader, the installed local recovery job remains preserved for the separately approved post-merge maintenance rollout, and PR #97 remains unchanged.

## 2026-07-18 - NFC browser pairing bootstrap correction observed result

- Confirmed the repeated uppercase `NFC_HELPER_PAIRING_CODE_INVALID` incident was browser-local validation before `fetch`: no helper, reader, tag, GoToTags, camera, lighting, printing, or inventory operation was initiated. The pre-existing route mismatch required a human to type a code that was deliberately hidden, the generic launcher page could not complete pairing, Finish discarded the fragment, and the NFC launcher delegated to the Windows default browser instead of the dedicated AI Grader Chrome profile.
- Removed manual pairing-code entry and manual browser-token clearing from the production page. The canonical flow now consumes the unexpired one-time fragment only in memory, scrubs it from the address immediately, authenticates through a reportless human-only `nfc-program` readiness endpoint, proves schema/programming/token/allowlist/protocol readiness, and only then contacts the fixed loopback helper. A successful generic launch redirects to Finish; exact-report continuation validates the saved helper token. Disabled/incomplete hosted readiness makes zero local-helper calls.
- A stale saved browser token can be replaced only after exact HTTP `401` / `workstation_token_invalid` plus a valid fresh in-memory shortcut code. The stale token is removed before `/pair`, is never sent to that route, and the replacement token is validated and persisted. Timeout, unavailability, malformed fragments, missing code, and unrelated errors never clear trust or trigger pairing. Consumed-code/wrong-profile state now reports that separately authorized pairing-code-only maintenance is required and that no code should be entered.
- Added report-generation guards and state resets so route changes, unmounts, concurrent refreshes, and later disabled readiness cannot reuse an earlier enabled connection or apply stale report/helper state. Active F8215 cleanup remains exact-attempt-only and does not contact the helper while hosted programming readiness is disabled or incomplete.
- The stable installed launcher now discovers Chrome explicitly, pins `C:\TenKings\chrome-ai-grader-profile`, rejects an unsafe/reparse profile leaf, uses `--new-window`, and never delegates to the default browser. Before opening Chrome it waits at most five seconds for `127.0.0.1:47662` to be owned by the exact helper `Win32_Process.ProcessId`; this is a listener-only observation and performs no HTTP, PC/SC, reader, or tag request. Its non-secret launcher marker and optional hidden code remain fragment-only, and its result contains neither URL nor code.
- Corrected `MASTER_PRODUCT_CONTEXT.md` to current evidence: the NFC schema/routes/configuration and programming policy are deployed, while NFC-required is temporarily disabled pending this reviewed browser/launcher correction and separately controlled workstation recovery.
- Hardware-free validation passed: focused NFC frontend/API/client/integration `46/46`; complete AI Grader/Google Vision frontend `194/194`; frontend TypeScript no-emit; Next `14.2.5` production build including `/ai-grader/nfc` and the new catch-all readiness action; all `26` tracked PowerShell parse checks; the complete NFC maintenance/path/ACL/template/update/rollback/quarantine/launcher suite; Next trace containment with zero native helper, PC/SC, GoToTags adapter, or maintenance implementation matches; and `git diff --check`. The isolated worktree dependency setup reused `667` offline pnpm packages with zero downloads, used only an unreachable process-scoped loopback Prisma placeholder for generation, and created no tracked dependency/lockfile change or cross-worktree junction.
- Production remains unblocked at exact source `cf3595b139f81adb735ba5a190a6ce5c23d61a57` and deployment `dpl_2b3zyZaVoURWwZoZ1QoyKCgDuZ35`, with `AI_GRADER_NFC_REQUIRED=false`; this software correction is review-only and was not merged or deployed. The installed Dell launcher/helper/config/token/pairing state/CNG key/task/ACLs were not changed. - Exact historical V0 replay: response `100*(max(channel)-min(channel))/max(mean(channel)+12,18)`; side normalization `clamp((response-p50)/(p95-p50),0,1)`; 12x16 cells ranked by `0.65*mean+0.35*max`; severity `rank*100*max(0.55,1-0.3*clippedFraction)`; high band at `>=68`; high-band deduction `1.40`. Front p50/p95/p99 were `15.6379/230.2368/346.9159`; back `19.8582/75.6757/218.5366`. All `16` candidates were high: nominal `22.40` was clamped to `4.50`, so Surface was exactly `10.00-4.50=5.50`. Weighted score was `0.30*10+0.25*8.97+0.25*8.97+0.20*5.50=8.585`; the any-high-surface cap produced reported overall `8.50`.
- The old mask called `glare-clipping` detects only saturation (`allOn>=248` or any channel `>=252`). It never excludes a pixel or suppresses a finding. Front candidates had `6.2195%-83.3791%` saturation overlap and still deducted. Back candidates had 0% overlap because the same illumination artifact remained below saturation and was amplified by the much lower back p95.
- Classification is directional, channel-selective ring/specular illumination plus algorithmic normalization/deduplication error, not classic all-channel common mode and not independently proven card damage. Zero candidate pixels had four or more channels simultaneously above their card P90; candidate common-mode means were at or below the side mean. Directional residual RMS was `29.8-71.0` front versus card `7.14`, and `16.25-22.54` back versus card `6.81`. Channel 4 dominates upper cells and Channel 3 lower cells on both sides; the other reported source channels are often the darkest channels because V0 ranks absolute deviation rather than corroborating bright evidence. All eight front cells are exact or one grid cell from a back cell, three are exact matches, and the upper-Channel-4/lower-Channel-3 pattern survives the flip. Combined with the immutable fixed-rig observation of a circular reflection, this supports a repeatable lighting/specular artifact. No calibrated ground truth or Dino-Lite evidence certifies any of these cells as physical damage.
- Root causes confirmed from source: the unified report omitted registered dark control for this run; accepted-profile input was unused; no per-channel flat-field, common-mode/residual separation, non-saturating specular mask, valid-pixel coverage, alternate-channel recovery, multi-channel corroboration, connected-component segmentation, overlap merge, or physical-defect deduplication existed. Heatmap pixels were the same derived response, not independent evidence. The global surface confidence average `0.745` was therefore misleading.
- Normalized production printed-design centering is intentionally `not_computed`; acquisition placement is excluded and no printed-border or approved registered-template detector exists. Missing centering redistributes its `0.30` weight over Corners/Edges/Surface, subtracts `0.18` confidence, and caps provisional overall at `9.00`.
- All four same-side corner and all four same-side edge scores use identical side-global `clippedPixelFraction`, `darkPixelFraction`, and `sharpnessScore`; ROI identity changes only labels/rectangles. Their V0 formula is `clamp(10-clipped*40-dark*8+min(sharpness,500)/250,0,10)`. This is an evidence-quality proxy, not independent physical-condition measurement. Provisional source and public schemas permit `0` while final promotion clamps to `1`, confirming the strict-score inconsistency.
- No evidence file, raw role, report, Production/staging database, storage object, helper, camera, lighting, NFC, label, inventory, Vercel configuration, deployment, migration, or PR #97 was accessed mutably or changed. Replay calculations were read-only and in memory; no temporary derived files remain.

## 2026-07-18 - Mathematical Calibration V1 centering geometry and registration-authority hardening observed result

- Planned and completed two hardware-free architecture corrections from the independent calibration review. Printed-border centering no longer treats each side as one median constant-axis coordinate: it now performs deterministic robust 2-D fitting across many samples, retains normalized line equations/slopes/intercepts/inliers/residuals, constructs the printed contour from the four exact side-line intersections, measures opposing margins at the calibrated center axes, and root-sum-square propagates the line-position U95 into the centering difference deadband. The source detector now tracks inset-ordered peaks and retains the same tilted-line evidence while preserving explicit ambiguity rejection for multiple fully supported boundaries.
- Registered-design centering no longer accepts caller-declared transform matrices, residuals, inlier counts, fractions, or confidence. The projection seam verifies the exact approved artifact bytes against the approved SHA-256 and the exact normalized source bytes against their SHA-256, canonicalizes and hashes an ordered correspondence ledger, computes affine or homography registration and robust inliers internally, hashes the computed registration result, and makes the centering seam independently reproduce the ledger hash, transform, metrics, inlier identities, result hash, and exact normalized-source evidence linkage before scoring. Tampered ledgers, transforms, metrics, hashes, mismatched evidence, insufficient controls, duplicate/out-of-frame controls, and degenerate geometry fail as insufficient evidence.
- The centralized threshold manifest and hash were not edited by this correction. Focused validation passed: capture-helper TypeScript build; registered-design authority tests `9/9`; printed-border source/tilt/ambiguity tests `5/5`; and physical-calibration/centering/corner/edge tests `14/14`. Scoped `git diff --check` passed.
- This work was offline and hardware-free. No target/card was positioned, no capture or lighting action occurred, and Production, databases, storage, installed helper, NFC, labels, inventory, Vercel, deployment, migration, and PR #97 remained untouched.

## 2026-07-18 - Mathematical Calibration V1 deterministic physical-finding deduplication observed result

- Removed caller authority from finding fusion. One fused component retains the strongest measurement-derived primary category and links secondary-category evidence without a second deduction.
- Added explicit front/back physical-match thresholds under both `corners.frontBackPhysicalMatch` and `edges.frontBackPhysicalMatch`: minimum normalized ROI box IoU `0.35`, maximum normalized centroid distance `0.05`, maximum relative effective-measurement difference `0.25`, and U95 interval multiplier `1`. A duplicate is removed only when mirrored physical location, exact primary category, measurement kind/unit, normalized geometry, and effective measurement all satisfy those gates. Candidate pairing and retained-highest-deduction selection are deterministic; physical IDs in the published ledger are composer-generated and unique rather than caller-authoritative.
- Each corner/edge finding now retains its normalized observation-ROI bounding box and centroid. The grade composer independently reproduces front/back matching rather than trusting the aggregate result's link ledger. - Fused surface evidence is rebound from seed-local region IDs to the final physical-finding region before immutable-authority deduplication. Repeated identical asset/hash/channel references now collapse once; a same asset/region/channel reference with conflicting hash, side, or role fails instead of using first-wins behavior. Regression coverage proves all measurement evidence points to the final region and the repeated authority appears exactly once.

## 2026-07-18 - Mathematical V1 local-station and Rapid Capture integration observed result

- Wired the exact finalized calibration-bundle readiness loader and fixed-rig Mathematical station adapter into the ordinary single-card and serialized Rapid Capture paths. Each session now persists explicit processing, finding_review_required, completed, or insufficient_evidence state with exact request hashes, review issues, failure stage, reasons, recapture/reference/calibration/implementation flags, attempt count, and v0FallbackUsed=false. Mathematical sessions never call the legacy diagnostic scorer or redistribute/fallback to a V0/manual grade.

The bridge persists the exact hash-bound review request and create-new copies of its normalized True View, eight directional channels, ROI crop, segmentation mask, confidence mask, and illumination mask under the exact private station session. A token-gated read-only endpoint re-hashes every asset before delivery. Operator submission is a complete per-finding confirmed/adjusted disposition bound to the exact request SHA-256; caller-authored confidence and partial/duplicate/stale reviews are rejected. An accepted complete review deterministically reruns the same generated-at/session/report/calibration/warm-manifest authority.
- Added a separate token-gated binary endpoint for approved registered-design artifacts. It requires exact session, side, reference ID, SHA-256, MIME type, and Content-Length headers, permits only PNG/JPEG through 64 MiB, writes inside the fixed session directory with exclusive-create semantics, verifies readback bytes/hash, rejects caller paths and query parameters, and re-verifies the staged bytes before capture. The browser cannot author registration transforms, residuals, confidence, publication certificate, report URL, or QR URL; the adapter computes registration and the bridge derives publication authority from the exact report ID.
- Mathematical completion remains on the existing release/Label V1/Approve & Publish path, but its release branch exclusively invokes writeAiGraderMathematicalProductionReleaseV1 (which invokes buildAiGraderMathematicalProductionReleaseV1). ## 2026-07-19 - Mathematical V1 automatic-registration fixture correction

- Planned action: diagnose the sole remaining automatic registered-design validation failure without lowering the centralized NCC, ambiguity, inlier, residual, or confidence gates and without accepting caller-authored registration data.
- Observed result: the registration implementation and acceptance gates were correct. The focused test fixture was not: Sharp expands a blurred one-channel raw raster to three channels unless grayscale output is requested, while the fixture re-declared that three-channel buffer as one channel both when building the reference and after source blur. That byte reinterpretation meant the supposed valid source was not an affine transform of its approved reference. The fixture now explicitly converts both blur outputs back to one grayscale channel before reading raw bytes. The two existing non-scoring `8 px` algorithm bounds are now explicit manifest fields (`minimumReferenceCandidateSeparationPx` and `maximumConsensusDisplacementResidualPx`) instead of being derived in source as twice the `4 px` patch radius; their behavior is unchanged. No NCC, ambiguity, inlier, residual, confidence, transform-authority, or production-source gate was weakened.
- Validation evidence: `pnpm --filter @tenkings/shared build` passed; `pnpm --filter @tenkings/ai-grader-capture-helper build` passed; and `pnpm --filter @tenkings/ai-grader-capture-helper exec node --test --test-concurrency=1 tests/fixedRigAutomaticDesignRegistrationV1.test.js` passed `3/3`, covering the noisy shifted affine success case, repeated/flat/wrong-reference rejection, and missing pixel/mm fail-closed behavior. Temporary diagnostic state and logging were removed.
- This work was offline and hardware-free. No target/card was positioned, no camera/light action occurred, and no Production database/storage/report/card, installed Dell helper/configuration, NFC/tag, physical label, inventory, Vercel/deployment, migration, merge, or PR #97 state was accessed or changed.
