- Post-rollout Production preview root cause was captured from an attached bridge process. Normal `/preview/stream` requests correctly omit the calibration-only `X-AI-Grader-Mathematical-Calibration-Session-Id` header, but `streamPreview` called the required-header parser unconditionally; the rejected async route was returned without `await`, escaped the HTTP server `try/catch`, and terminated port `47652` with exit code `1`. Three operator attempts reached `capture_front` with `5/5` positioning-light acknowledgements but zero preview frames and zero captures. Surgical correction: the calibration header is optional for normal Production preview and remains exact when supplied; the HTTP route now awaits the stream so request failures return through the bounded error handler instead of killing the bridge. ## 2026-07-21 - AI Grader admin report-editor server boundary

- Added an admin-only, no-migration report-editor API at `/api/admin/ai-grader/report-editor/{state,save,visibility}`. It preserves the immutable published bundle and the machine-owned `AiGraderReport.finalOverallGrade`, `elementScores`, `AiGraderGrade`, label, and publish-authority records. The active human-reviewed adjudication is stored separately at `AiGraderReport.gradeStory.manualReportRevision`.
- Every mutation uses the existing report-lifecycle advisory lock, an optimistic revision token bound to the exact source bundle SHA-256, a required private reason, and a canonical chained `AuditEvent` full snapshot. Invalid active revisions, broken audit chains, stale tokens, contradictory publish manifests, and source-hash mismatches fail explicitly; the machine report is never silently substituted for a broken adjudication.
- A manifest/hash-bound known-schema machine-failure bundle may enter Edit Mode even when it cannot satisfy the normal public report sanitizer, but only when it explicitly reports failure/incomplete evidence and does not claim successful final grading. The API never returns that raw bundle. Saving all four admin-confirmed subgrades creates `operator_adjudication_v1` with `completionStatus=human_reviewed_complete`, server-owned Mathematical V1 calculation, preserved source severe-defect cap, and the adjudicated machine-failure codes.

## 2026-07-21 - Hosted calibration authority authenticity correction

- Closed the browser-carried-authority defect by making both pending and ACTIVE/start authority canonical P-256/SHA-256 IEEE-P1363 signed envelopes. Hosted owns only the PKCS#8 private key; each envelope binds phase, activation/snapshot/rig identity, every calibration/context hash, event revision, timestamps/expiry, and the ACTIVE workstation-receipt hash.
- The real Dell helper now requires a rig-scoped, SHA-256-keyed DER-SPKI allowlist. It verifies the hosted signature before writing PENDING, before changing the local pointer ACTIVE, and on every Start. Unsigned legacy, unknown-key, malformed, wrong-phase, tampered, expired-pending/Start, replayed, cross-rig, and cross-activation values hard-fail without pointer fallback.
- ACTIVE envelopes are short-lived for new Start authorization. A card already authorized and bound may finish processing after that Start credential expires, but still rechecks the exact signature, ACTIVE pointer, immutable receipt/bundle, and live operating context. New cards must fetch a fresh hosted envelope.
- Hosted activation requests fail before transaction mutation when signing configuration is absent or invalid. Real helper construction fails before key/inventory reads when pinned hosted public keys are absent. The private signing key is never returned; the browser remains transport only.
- Exported hosted route/action names and DTO names remain unchanged. New configuration is `AI_GRADER_CALIBRATION_HOSTED_AUTHORITY_SIGNING_KEY_PKCS8_BASE64` on hosted and `AI_GRADER_CALIBRATION_HOSTED_AUTHORITY_PUBLIC_KEYS_JSON` on the helper.
- Hardware-free validation: shared/database/helper builds and Next.js typecheck passed; database activation/snapshot/production `67/67`, serialized helper selection `29/29`, finalizer `3/3`, hosted API `6/6`, and `git diff --check` passed.
